word ook lid van de vereniging van volksbeschermers en help ons mee ambtsdelicten aan te pakken: https://volksbeschermers.nl


Momenteel heeft de overheid er voor gezorgd dat ik nergens kan werken (AIVD/NCTV zet werkgevers onder druk) en dat mijn stichting mijn goede naam op alle manieren wordt aangevallen door corrupte overheidsjournalisten.



Als jullie mij willen helpen zodat ik meer slagkracht heb, dan kan je doneren aan mij en mijn stichting zodat we door kunnen gaan met de video's online om de overheidscorruptie aan te pakken?  

Beste strijder
Dit is een betaalverzoek voor Sponsor bijdragen Stichting . Je mag zelf het bedrag kiezen. Je kunt met elke bank in Nederland betalen. Dank je wel! 

Deze link is geldig tot 20 maart 2026.
https://betaalverzoek.rabobank.nl/betaalverzoek/?id=sDenMa4xRSKTk05kpVHH2A

Ontzettend bedankt voor je steun en laat even een berichtje achter.

Bekijk ook de rest van de video's op mijn kanaal over overheids en media corruptie in Nederland.

Bezoek ook onze website https://modellenstichting.nl over grooming, uitbuiting en dat de modellenbranche het grootste mensenhandel netwerk is in NL waar de overheid medeplichtig aan is geworden met hun corruptie justitie en veiligheid.


Waarom ziet het er zo uit en waarom hebben ze gekozen voor zo'n goedkoop uitziend en slecht onderhouden graf? 

Wat is hier nou exact aan de hand?

Heeft dit iets met Mabel Wisse Smit te maken? Of andere conspiracy theorieen welke misschien wel kloppen?!

snippet.title: Wegen Mordes: Lenkerin (18) bekommt lebenslängliche Haft | krone.tv NEWS
snippet.description: Im Juli vergangenen Jahres fuhr die damals 17-jährige Mackenzie, ohne abzubremsen, mit 160 km/h gegen ein Backsteingebäude. Bei dem Autounfall kamen ihr damaliger Freund Dominic Russo (20) sowie ein weiterer Freund, Davion Flanagan (19), ums Leben. Die Burschen waren auf der Stelle tot. Nur die Teenagerin überlebte. Dass es gar kein Unfall war, kam bald heraus: Eine Überwachungskamera zeichnete auf, wie die Lenkerin kurz dem Aufprall sogar noch auf das Gaspedal trat und beschleunigte.  Alle drei Insassen standen unter dem Einfluss von Marihuana. Auch Magic Mushrooms seien im Auto entdeckt worden.

Richard is a renowned stock market analyst and founder of Arete Research, an independent equity research firm, which works with over 250 global tech investors.

- The process Richard follows to decide which tech innovations to invest in
- What investors really need to know about tech to be Smart Money

If you ever leave me

I'm so in love with you

The birds in the sky would be sad and lonely

If you're bad to me

You're not bad
To me
To me
To me


--
🎶 Song Info:

"Bad to Me" is a song credited to Lennon–McCartney. In late interviews, John Lennon said that he wrote it for Billy J. Kramer with The Dakotas[2] while on holiday in Spain. However, in a 1964 interview he said that he and Paul McCartney wrote it in the back of a van, declaring McCartney a contributor.[3] Bootlegs exist of Lennon's original demo of the song, which was recorded on 31 May 1963. An acoustic demo from the same era was released on iTunes in December 2013 on the album The Beatles Bootleg Recordings 1963. It became one of the first occasions a Lennon–McCartney composition made the US Top 40 recorded by an artist other than the Beatles (the first being "A World Without Love" by Peter & Gordon; another being "Goodbye" by Mary Hopkin).

Label: Parlophone – R 5049
Format: Vinyl, 7", 45 RPM, Single
Country: UK
Released: 1963
Genre: Rock, Pop
Style: Vocal, Beat

B-side: "I Call Your Name"
Released: 26 July 1963 (UK) May 1964 (US)
Recorded: 26 June 1963
Genre: Pop, beat
Length: 2:22
Label: Parlophone R5049
Songwriter(s): Lennon–McCartney
Producer(s): George Martin

William Ashton was discovered by Brian Epstein and paired with Manchester-based group The Dakotas. William's performing name, Kramer, was chosen at random from a telephone directory. John Lennon suggested adding the "J" for a "tougher edge". Kramer was given a series of songs specially written for him by John Lennon and Paul McCartney which launched him into stardom. "I'll Keep You Satisfied", "From a Window", "I Call Your Name" and "Bad to Me". They also released "Do You Want to Know a Secret?" reaching #2 backed by "I'll Be on My Way" which was unreleased by the Beatles. The Dakotas enjoyed Top 20 success in 1963 on their own. "Little Children" was their biggest hit reaching #2 in the USA.

snippet.title: brian epstein's screentime on "hullabaloo"
snippet.description:
Hullabaloo was an American musical variety series that ran on NBC from 1965 to 1966. Its first thirteen episodes featured a segment called “Hullabaloo London” in which Beatles’ manager Brian Epstein introduced an up-and-coming UK act. Here, I’ve cobbled together all the footage of him across these episodes.

Timestamps for each episode:
0:00 Episode 1: Gerry and the Pacemakers "I'll Be There" and "It's Gonna Be Alright" (January 12).
01:05 Episode 2: Marianne Faithfull "As Tears Go By” (January 19).
02:52 Episode 3: Freddie and the Dreamers “I’m Telling You Now” and “I Understand” (January 26).
04:19 Episode 4: Cliff Bennett and the Rebel Rousers “One Way Love” (February 2).
05:32 Episode 5: Herman’s Hermits “I’m Into Something Good” and “Can’t You Hear My Heartbeat” and interview with Richard Lester (February 9).
08:40 Episode 6: Band of Angels “Not True as Yet” and interview with Andrew Loog-Oldham (February 16).
12:16 Episode 7: The Searchers "What Have They Done to the Rain" and "Love Potion Number 9" (February 23).
13:11 Episode 8: Wayne Fontana & The Mindbenders “The Game of Love” (March 2).
14:07 Episode 9: Billy J. Kramer & the Dakotas “It’s Gonna Last Forever” and “The Twelfth of Never” (March 9).
16:01 Episode 10: Georgie Fame & The Blue Flames “Point Of No Return” and “Yeh Yeh” (March 16).
17:59 Episode 11: The Moody Blues “Go Now” (March 23). I may be missing an outro section for this one but I can’t find a full version of the episode to know for sure.
18:25 Episode 12: Joe Brown & The Bruvvers “Teardrops In The Rain” and “The Spanish Bit” (March 30).
--:--Episode 13: Marianne Faithfull “Come And Stay With Me” (April 6). This was a “Top Pops” episode that used footage from the same filming session as Marianne’s prior appearance on episode 2. Brian is referenced but not shown in episode 13; I suspect a second interview segment with him and Marianne was cut for time.

snippet.title: Penn & Teller on David Blaine
snippet.description:
Click here for the FULL interview https://youtu.be/YRf8YTnqUfA 

This is a podcast interview of Penn & Teller, as they share their thoughts on David Blaine and other magicians. 

#davidblaine 

What did you think of this video titled Penn & Teller on David Blaine let us know in the comments below. 

Penn and Teller have expressed admiration for David Blaine's magic and performance style, while also acknowledging their differences in approach and philosophy.

In an interview with The Guardian in 2019, Penn Jillette praised David Blaine as "one of the most dedicated performers out there," noting his willingness to push the boundaries of what is possible in magic and his ability to engage audiences with his stunts and illusions.

However, Penn also noted that he and Teller have a different approach to magic than Blaine, who often emphasizes the mysterious and otherworldly aspects of the art form. Penn and Teller, on the other hand, tend to be more straightforward in their presentations and often reveal the secrets behind their tricks.

In a 2014 interview with The Telegraph, Teller expressed similar sentiments about David Blaine, describing him as "an amazing performer" but also noting that he and Penn have a different philosophy when it comes to magic. Teller explained that he and Penn see magic as a form of storytelling, and that they prioritize clarity and coherence in their performances over the mere appearance of mystery.

Despite these differences, Penn and Teller have often spoken respectfully of David Blaine and his contributions to the art of magic. They have also collaborated with him on occasion, such as when Blaine appeared on an episode of their television show, Penn & Teller: Fool Us, in 2017.

snippet.title: S.01 EP.02 YOU SOUND LIKE MY F*ING MOTHER with Street Photographer Jill Freedman
snippet.description:
JILL FREEDMAN, 1939 - 2019. A legend of street photography, Jill traveled the world dedicating her long life to the still image. Her books was the pride of her enormous body of inspiring work. These influential art books have a wide range of topics reflecting her insatiable curiosity; 1970’s NYC Police, the traveling circus, firefighters, rural Ireland and so much more. These were often grueling, self-funded projects to insure total control. Like many true artists, all of Jills interests laid in making the work rather than promoting it. Despite respected publications, gallery shows, and validation from her peers, many would agree she was under-appreciated in her life.
